title: Production Operations, Incidents, Recovery, and Release Defense · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A product-security pair incrementally builds cryptographic production defense and recovery drill, changes one attacker-controlled input, and keeps earlier Go, accessibility, secret-lifecycle, testing, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of cryptographic production defense and recovery drill while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Define low-cardinality success, reject, latency, work-factor, key-version, algorithm, expiry, rotation, migration, dependency, and recovery signals without secret leakage.
Load-test entropy, CPU, memory-hard KDFs, HSM/KMS quotas, certificate services, revocation, key caches, queues, and degraded modes with bounded admission.
Canary reader/writer, algorithm, key, format, certificate, and dependency changes with old/new matrices, dual-read boundaries, telemetry, and rollback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Observability Slos
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 3711 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate release on deterministic, vector, fuzz, interoperability, native side-channel, load, rollback, and recovery evidence; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Observability Slos.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define low-cardinality success, reject, latency, work-factor, key-version, algorithm, expiry, rotation, migration, dependency, and recovery signals without secret leakage. For Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Observability Slos, keep Crypto Go Observability Slos connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

workshop case — A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 5330 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend threat model, primitive choice, parameters, implementation, key custody, tests, interoperability, side channels, compliance limits, rollout, rollback, recovery, and residual risk before release.
